label: true-dup
a: GraphQL for the internal frontend (our own team). REST for the external public API. GraphQL flexibility benefits our own frontend but the learning curve is a barrier for external developers who expect REST.
b: Dual API strategy: internal-facing API is GraphQL — the frontend team can request exactly the data shape they need. External public API is REST — third-party developers have a lower integration barrier and all their existing tooling works without special clients.
reviewer_notes: Both describe the same internal-GraphQL / external-REST split with the same rationale.
